channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Com Lula em 8º, confira o ranking dos presidentes mais populares da América Latina
item.description: Lula (PT) ranks as the eighth most popular president in Latin America in March 2026, with 46.6% positive image, according to a CB Consultora Opinión Pública survey. He dropped from sixth place in February, while Claudia Sheinbaum (Mexico) leads the ranking with 72.3% approval. The first South American on the list is Bolivian Rodrigo Paz, ranked sixth overall with 51.9%.

item.title: Folha secreta do Ceperj: entenda denúncia contra Cláudio Castro no TSE
item.description: Former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL) faces trial at the TSE over alleged use of a 'secret payroll' at Ceperj and Uerj during the 2022 campaign, involving 27,000 and 18,000 temporary positions respectively to hire election operatives. Castro resigned one day before the trial resumed, asserting confidence in electoral justice and denying wrongdoing after the campaign began. The MP-RJ reports a 2,139% increase in Ceperj spending from 2020 to 2022, which could result in ineligibility until 2030 if convicted.

item.title: O que resta para o TSE concluir julgamento que pode tornar Cláudio Castro inelegível
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) resumes the trial that could render former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro ineligible for eight years, based on the use of public structure in the 2022 campaign. So far, two ministers have voted for conviction, with votes still pending from Kassio Nunes Marques, Floriano de Azevedo Marques, Estela Aranha, André Mendonça, and President Cármen Lúcia. The case centers on allegations of 27,000 temporary hires at the state-funded Fundação Ceperj used to boost Castro’s re-election bid, allegedly constituting an illegal diversion of public resources for electoral gain.

item.title: Alta do IOF faz governo Lula arrecadar 41,8% a mais com o tributo
item.description: The Lula government recorded a 41.8% increase in IOF tax revenue in the first two months of 2026, reaching R$16.8 billion, driven by higher rates and an expanded tax base under Decree 12.499/25. Despite opposition from Congress and financial markets, the government preserved most of the increase following STF rulings, with Justice Alexandre de Moraes upholding the decree and rejecting the congressional repeal. The IOF remains a key tool to strengthen federal finances amid slowing revenues from foreign trade.

item.title: Conselho do FGTS aprova ampliação de renda do Minha Casa Minha Vida
item.description: The FGTS Council approved the expansion of income brackets for the Minha Casa Minha Vida program, allowing families earning up to R$13,000 to participate. The changes include raising income limits across all brackets and increasing the maximum financed property value, benefiting around 127,000 families through lower interest rates and new eligibility.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula faz entrega do Minha Casa, Minha Vida Rural no Planalto
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva is participating in the simultaneous delivery of 2,215 homes under the Minha Casa, Minha Vida Rural program, with a ceremony at the Palácio do Planalto and live broadcast to Santarém (PA), Dias d’Avila (BA), Rio Largo (AL), and São Braz (AL). The largest batch, comprising 1,408 units, will be delivered in Santarém with the presence of Minister of Cities Jader Filho. The event takes place on Tuesday, March 24, 2026, at 11:00 a.m., underscoring the government’s commitment to rural public housing.

item.title: Saída de Castro reconfigura disputa no Rio e embaralha plano bolsonarista
item.description: The resignation of Governor Cláudio Castro reconfigures the Bolsonarist strategy in Rio, following an STF decision that barred Douglas Ruas from running for state governor due to insufficient disaffiliation. Castro aims to avoid ineligibility by resigning early, preserving his Senate candidacy. The Bolsonarist group has not yet chosen a new gubernatorial candidate, while allies of Eduardo Paes see an opportunity to field a competitive alternative.

item.title: O que está por trás do terno marrom que Lula passou a usar em eventos
item.description: President Lula drew attention by wearing a brown suit at a recent event at the Palácio do Planalto, breaking from his usual preference for somber tones like navy, black, and gray. The choice aligns with Pantone’s global selection of 'Mocha Mousse' as the Color of the Year, which has boosted the shade’s visibility in fashion, and also reflects his broader image repositioning following a professional weight-loss journey. The outfit was worn during the announcement of measures to mitigate fuel price impacts amid international tensions over oil.

item.title: Lula deve vetar trecho e sancionar restante do PL Antifacção, segundo auxiliares
item.description: President Lula is expected to sign the Anti-Crime Bill into law with only one veto, removing a provision that would have extended penalties to individuals associated with criminal factions, due to concerns over the criminalization of social movements. The signing ceremony will take place this Tuesday at 3 p.m. at the Planalto Palace, attended by Chamber President Hugo Motta, while Senate President Davi Alcolumbre is invited but not expected to attend. Simultaneously, the government is preparing a decree with six strategic axes to regulate the law, though its release has been postponed to next week.

item.title: Governo de SP amplia monitoramento ambiental por satélite
item.description: The São Paulo state government, under Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicans), expanded its environmental monitoring capacity ninefold using satellite imagery and geospatial analysis to detect changes in native vegetation and guide enforcement actions. The initiative aims to strengthen environmental protection across the state.

item.title: Ronaldo Caiado se reúne com Kassab em São Paulo após Ratinho Jr. desistir de disputar a Presidência
item.description: Goias Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) met with PSD national president Gilberto Kassab in São Paulo following the withdrawal of Ratinho Júnior from the party's presidential nomination race. Caiado has gained traction as a potential candidate after Ratinho Júnior's exit, while Eduardo Leite also vies for the position. Although not a consensus, Caiado is viewed by party figures as the likely favorite due to his experience, stance against seeking re-election, and strong internal polling.

item.title: Designer da camisa da seleção diz que torcedor chama Brasil de Brasa
item.description: Nike designer Rachel Denti explained that the term 'Brasa' on Brazil's 2026 World Cup jerseys is something she claims to hear in stadiums and on the streets, though fans criticized it as not being a common way to refer to Brazil. The inclusion sparked controversy on social media, with some users linking the term to left-wing politics and the Lula government.

item.title: Prefeito e primeira-dama do interior de SP morrem em acidente de carro
item.description: The mayor of Vista Alegre do Alto, Nelson Rozani (Podemos), and his wife, First Lady Fátima Rozani, died in a car accident on Monday night. The couple left three children and were mourned with official mourning declared by the city hall. Governor Tarcísio de Freitas expressed sorrow and solidarity with the family and the community.

item.title: Após desistência de Ratinho Jr, Kassab se encontra com Ronaldo Caiado em SP para falar de candidatura à presidência
item.description: Following Ratinho Júnior’s withdrawal from the presidential race, PSD national president Gilberto Kassab met with Goiás governor Ronaldo Caiado in São Paulo to discuss the party’s presidential nominee. Kassab also plans to meet with Rio Grande do Sul governor Eduardo Leite, who recently joined the PSD and has expressed interest in leading the ticket. The party aims to announce its candidate by the end of March, emphasizing a centrist alternative beyond political polarization.

item.title: Romário critica Ancelotti e defende Neymar na seleção: ‘Craque tem que jogar’
item.description: Romário criticized Carlo Ancelotti's decision to leave Neymar out of the Brazil national team, stating that 'a star player must play' and that Neymar's talent should not be wasted, even if not at peak fitness. Ancelotti insisted Neymar would only be considered if 100% physically fit, following recent knee issues. The former player affirmed he would support Brazil regardless, but reiterated his wish to see Neymar at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: JOTA Principal: Mendonça prorroga CPMI do INSS e fala em ‘omissão’ da cúpula do Congresso
item.description: Supreme Court Minister André Mendonça granted a preliminary injunction extending the INSS CPMI, giving Senate President Davi Alcolumbre 48 hours to read the extension request — a step sufficient to renew the investigation. He criticized Congress leadership for its 'omission' in failing to advance the probe. Senator Carlos Viana, CPMI president, praised the ruling as a constitutional victory and plans to call new witnesses, including bank representatives linked to payroll loan complaints.

item.title: Lula deve sancionar PL Antifacção nesta terça-feira
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva is expected to sign the Anti-Organized Crime Bill on Tuesday (24), which increases penalties for criminal organization participation and introduces new offenses such as 'structured social domain'. The bill includes provisions for extraordinary asset seizure without conviction and voting restrictions for pretrial detainees, drawing criticism from human rights groups over potential constitutional violations.

item.title: Senador bolsonarista é favorito para governar o estado mais forte do agronegócio, diz pesquisa
item.description: Senator Wellington Fagundes (PL), an ally of Jair Bolsonaro, is the favorite to govern Mato Grosso in 2026, according to Real Time Big Data poll, with 37% to 43% voting intention in the first round. He leads in all second-round scenarios, defeating opponents by margins of up to 35 percentage points. Other right-aligned candidates — Otaviano Pivetta (Republicanos), Jayme Campos (União Brasil) and Natasha Shlessarenko (PSD) — trail behind, with Natasha being the only one showing recent rapprochement with the Lula government.

item.title: Decisões de Ratinho e Castro visam salvar sucessores e podem prejudicar PL, avaliam bolsonaristas
item.description: Ratinho Júnior abandoned his presidential pre-candidacy to focus on reelection in Paraná, while Cláudio Castro resigned as Rio de Janeiro governor to avoid potential ineligibility. Bolsonarists criticize Ratinho for underestimating the PL, which strengthened its position by federating Sergio Moro to support Flávio Bolsonaro and weaken the PSD. The strategy aims to secure allied successors and maintain political control in both states amid electoral risks.

item.title: Globo de Cristal coroa temporada perfeita de Lucas Braathen pelo Brasil
item.description: Lucas Pinheiro Braathen won the Giant Slalom Crystal Globe at the FIS Alpine Ski World Cup in Lillehammer, Norway, becoming the first Brazilian ever to claim the title in the discipline. With 547 points, he overtook Swiss skier Marco Odermatt, who crashed out of the first run, securing the overall victory. The triumph capped a historic season in which Braathen also won Brazil’s first-ever Winter Olympic gold medal at Milano-Cortina 2026.

item.title: Sem imunidade e sob pressão do calendário eleitoral, delação de Vorcaro vira xadrez no STF e na PF
item.description: PGR and PF investigators are negotiating terms for a potential plea bargain by Daniel Vorcaro, stressing there is zero chance of criminal immunity and that any deal must bring new, verifiable facts. There is concern about not tying the process to the electoral calendar to avoid accusations of political interference, with assessments that delays could push the agreement past the elections. In the STF, the case is viewed as an institutional chess game, with possible referral to the full plenary by Minister André Mendonça, which could shift the voting balance.

item.title: Após abandono de Ratinho Junior, Caiado viaja a SP para decidir futuro presidencial com Kassab
item.description: Following Ratinho Junior's unexpected withdrawal from the PSD's presidential race, Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado flew to São Paulo to discuss with Gilberto Kassab whether he will become the party's new presidential candidate for 2026. Caiado seeks to confirm the party's genuine backing, having previously been treated as a secondary option, while Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite remains a fallback alternative. The episode has reset the PSD's candidate selection process to an early stage, despite prior investments in infrastructure for Junior's campaign.

item.title: Os motivos para o recuo de Ratinho Jr. da disputa pela presidência
item.description: Ratinho Jr. abandoned his presidential candidacy to avoid losing control of Paraná amid the threat posed by Sergio Moro and the lack of unity in the PSD, which is already signaling support for Lula in parts of the country. His decision prioritized securing a state successor and maintaining his local political base over entering an uncertain and weakened national race.

item.title: BC vê Selic em patamar elevado por mais tempo, diz ata do Copom
item.description: The Central Bank held the Selic rate at 14.75% per year after a 0.25 percentage point cut, stating that monetary policy will need to be tighter and longer-lasting due to uncertainty from Middle East conflicts and their potential inflationary impact. The Copom minutes emphasize that unanchored expectations require greater caution, with inflation projected at 4.17% for 2026, above the 3% target. The market awaits the appointment of Guilherme Mello to the Economic Policy Directorate, while two Copom seats remain vacant.

item.title: ​​Último dia para ​​Lula decidir sobre PL Antifacção​​
item.description: President Lula has until this Tuesday, the 24th, to decide on the sanction of the bill known as the 'Anti-Faction Law,' approved by Congress. The text may still be sanctioned today, although partial vetoes are possible due to pressure from entities advocating for changes. The final deadline for the presidential decision ends this Tuesday.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro lidera em MT com 46%, aponta pesquisa Realtime/Bigdata; Lula tem 30%
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro leads the presidential race in Mato Grosso with 46% of voting intentions, according to a Realtime/Bigdata poll, while Lula trails with 30%. The data reflects strong electoral preference in the state for candidates aligned with Bolsonarism, indicating a competitive landscape for the October elections. The survey was conducted among voters in the state and focuses on the national presidential scenario.

item.title: Justiça impede governo de SP de vender prédio de pesquisas recém reformado
item.description: São Paulo's Justice suspended the sale of the Sucen building in the city center following a public civil action by the Association of Scientific Researchers of the State of São Paulo. The court ruled that the recently renovated property, which hosts essential epidemiological surveillance and public health research linked to the Pasteur Institute, requires prior technical studies, community consultation, and legislative approval. The judge noted a possible violation of Article 272 of the state Constitution regarding the sale of scientific patrimony.

item.title: Caiado não é a terceira via, mas via auxiliar de Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Polls indicate that Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro are leading in voting intentions for the 2026 runoff, with a tight race for the center voter, who tends to vote based on rejection of one candidate. Caiado does not emerge as a competitive third way, but as an auxiliary option that could strengthen Bolsonaro’s base by drawing anti-Lula votes without directly threatening his position.

item.title: BTG Pactual anuncia indicados para prêmio de música brasileira
item.description: BTG Pactual announces the nominees for the 2026 BTG Pactual Brazilian Music Prize in an event hosted by Fábio Porchat, honoring singer Cazuza. The event features performances by Ney Matogrosso, Yago Oproprio, Joyce Alane, Bruna Alimonda, Almério, and Núbia, with reinterpretations of Cazuza's songs, and reveals nominees across 18 music categories.

item.title: Governo sanciona lei que permite venda de medicamentos em supermercados; veja regras
item.description: President Lula signed a law allowing the sale of medicines in supermarkets, provided there is a separate pharmacy area with licensed pharmacists present during all operating hours. The law sets specific rules for controlled substances, prohibiting their sale in open or non-separated areas, and permits digital channels for logistics and delivery, as long as sanitary regulations are fully respected.

item.title: Caiado e Kassab se reúnem em momento decisivo para PSD definir candidatura
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) and PSD President Gilberto Kassab meet in São Paulo to define the party's presidential candidacy following Ratinho Júnior's withdrawal, who chose to remain as governor of Paraná. Caiado is considered the favorite to represent the PSD against Lula (PT) and Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), with the party planning to announce its choice by end of March, aiming to position itself as an alternative to radical polarization.

item.title: Zema manda recado a ‘intocáveis de Brasília’, ao iniciar pré-campanha ao Planalto
item.description: Romeu Zema, former governor of Minas Gerais and pre-candidate for President from the Novo party, announced the start of his pre-campaign, emphasizing he will travel across the country by car and without luxuries to differentiate himself from Brasília politicians. He denied any plans to join Gilberto Kassab’s PSD and reaffirmed his commitment to ending privileges, stating that the 'untouchables of Brasília' fear a candidate who does not live in palaces or use corporate cards.

item.title: Caiado candidato seria um estorvo para a campanha de Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: The likely presidential candidacy of Ronaldo Caiado from the PSD is expected to split the right-wing electorate and hinder Flávio Bolsonaro's campaign, as both candidates target voters prioritizing federal action on public security. Caiado benefits from high approval in Goiás due to his state-level security policies and strong agribusiness oligarchy, having also defeated Jair Bolsonaro's endorsed candidates in the 2024 municipal elections. Polls show he leads over Ratinho Junior and enjoys broad support among right-wing voters, both bolsonarist and non-bolsonarist, making him a direct and potentially obstructive rival to Flávio Bolsonaro.

item.title: Filha de Silvio Santos surpreende, se filia a partido e aceita ser candidata
item.description: Silvia Abravanel, daughter of Silvio Santos, has joined the PSD and accepted to run as a federal deputy candidate in the 2026 elections. The decision was made after a meeting with the party leadership, without consulting her sisters, and comes amid her family's political legacy, as her father ran for president in 1989. She hosts the children's show 'Sábado Animado' on SBT and leads the Brazilian Association for Obesity Combat (ABCO).

item.title: TSE retoma julgamento que pode deixar Cláudio Castro inelegível
item.description: The TSE resumed a trial that could render former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro ineligible until 2030 for alleged abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections, including the use of public funds to hire campaign workers via Ceperj and UERJ. Despite his resignation, the case proceeds with a current 2-0 vote in favor of conviction, and may also affect ALERJ president Rodrigo Bacellar and vice-governor Thiago Pampolha.

item.title: TSE volta a julgar Cláudio Castro nesta terça
item.description: The TSE will resume on Tuesday the trial of former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL), accused of political and economic power abuse in the 2022 elections. The partial score stands at 2-0 in favor of mandate annulment and ineligibility, after the session was previously interrupted.

item.title: Os números da disputa pela presidência entre os eleitores do Mato Grosso, segundo nova pesquisa
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro leads the presidential race in Mato Grosso with 45-46% voter intention across three scenarios, followed by Lula at 30-31%. Despite Lula having the highest rejection rate (60%) in the state — reflecting the region's right-wing strength — Flávio Bolsonaro also faces significant rejection (38%). The survey, based on 1,600 interviews conducted between March 21 and 23, has a margin of error of two percentage points.

item.title: O pedido de Renata Abreu para que o Podemos apoie Flávio para a Presidência
item.description: The national president of Podemos, Renata Abreu, asked Flávio Bolsonaro for his support so that her party would back her Senate campaign, linking this support to a potential 2026 presidential bid by Flávio. Flávio expressed sympathy for the idea but stated that the decision on São Paulo’s Senate seat rests with former deputy Eduardo Bolsonaro, who is currently in the U.S. The PL is expected to field its own candidate in one Senate slot and support Guilherme Derrite (PP) in the other, with Mário Frias seen as Eduardo’s preferred choice.

item.title: Bolsonarista Wellington Fagundes lidera disputa pelo governo de Mato Grosso, aponta pesquisa
item.description: Bolsonarist senator Wellington Fagundes (PL) leads all first- and second-round scenarios for the Mato Grosso governorship, according to a Real Time Big Data poll. He consistently outperforms rivals including Otaviano Pivetta (Republicanos), Jayme Campos (União Brasil), and Natasha Slhessarenko (PSD). Incumbent governor Mauro Mendes (União Brasil) enjoys a 72% approval rating, positively influencing perceptions of the state government.

item.title: Brasileirão 2026 já soma oito técnicos demitidos em oito rodadas
item.description: The 2026 Brasileirão has already seen eight coaching dismissals in just eight rounds, marking the worst start to a season in the last four editions. Martín Anselmi's firing by Botafogo after a win over Bragantino capped a list that includes Sampaoli, Diniz, Osorio, Filipe Luís, Crespo, Tite, and Vojvoda, some of which were controversial despite positive results. The FIFA Date pause should temporarily halt this trend, with the 9th round resuming only on April 1st.

item.title: Eduardo Cunha quer voltar a ser presidente da Câmara
item.description: Former federal deputy Eduardo Cunha (Republicanos-MG) aims to return to Congress in 2026 from Minas Gerais and is already eyeing the presidency of the Chamber of Deputies in 2027. He has been in talks with leaders of Centrão parties such as PSD, PL, MDB, and União to build support for his political comeback.

item.title: Cláudio Castro diz que sai do governo do Rio "de cabeça erguida"
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) resigned from office with the intention to run for Senate in 2026, stating he leaves 'with head held high'. His resignation comes on the eve of the resumption of a TSE trial that could cassate his mandate for abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 campaign, potentially making him ineligible for eight years. Following his resignation, the president of the Rio de Janeiro Court of Justice assumes interim leadership and must call an indirect election among state deputies to select a temporary governor until the October 2026 general elections.

item.title: Lula faz 1ª reunião com Durigan como ministro
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva met on Tuesday morning (March 24, 2026) with the new Finance Minister, Dario Durigan, in Brasília, marking the president’s first engagement of the day. The meeting also included Management Minister Esther Dweck and secretaries Guilherme Mello and Bruno Moretti. Durigan took over after Fernando Haddad resigned to run for governor of São Paulo in the October elections, as part of a gradual ministerial reshuffle aimed at strengthening the government’s political base in an election year.

item.title: Caiado se reúne com Kassab para definir pré-candidatura do PSD
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) travels to São Paulo to meet with PSD national president Gilberto Kassab to define the party’s 2026 presidential pre-candidacy. Following the withdrawal of Paraná Governor Ratinho Junior, Caiado has become the party’s favored candidate, while Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite remains an alternative and considers running for Senate as a backup. The meeting aims to finalize the PSD’s presidential nominee, with an announcement expected by Friday, March 27, 2026.

item.title: TSE retoma nesta 3ª julgamento de Cláudio Castro
item.description: The TSE resumes the trial concerning former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL), former vice-governor Thiago Pampolha (MDB), and former Alerj president Rodrigo Bacellar (União Brasil), with the current vote count at 2-0 in favor of cassation. The case examines alleged abuse of political and economic power during the 2022 elections through temporary hires via Ceperj and Uerj, allegedly used to boost Castro’s re-election campaign. Despite Castro’s resignation, the trial proceeds due to potential consequences including eight-year ineligibility and diploma annulment.

item.title: TSE decide nesta terça-feira o futuro político de Cláudio Castro no Rio
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) resumes on Tuesday the trial that could render former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL) ineligible, after he resigned to run for Senate. Castro’s allies argue his resignation removes the legal subject of the case, invalidating any ruling, but jurists and opponents contest this, saying the trial should proceed. So far, two ministers have voted for his disqualification, with five more yet to vote.

item.title: Moro se filia ao PL após idas e vindas com o bolsonarismo
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro (União Brasil-PR) announced his affiliation with the Liberal Party (PL) to run for governor of Paraná in the 2026 elections, after facing obstacles with the PP in the state. This move marks his return to the Bolsonaro family's political circle, as he was chosen by Jair Bolsonaro in 2018 to serve as Justice Minister, a position he left following conflicts over the anti-crime package, the transfer of Coaf, and the replacement of the Federal Police director-general, which Moro accused of being an attempt to interfere in investigations involving Bolsonaro's family.

item.title: Gayer brinca sobre dança de Flávio: “Parece um orangotango”
item.description: Federal deputy Gustavo Gayer (PL-GO) posted a video joking about Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL-RJ) dance moves, comparing them to an orangutan and to Donald Trump's campaign dance style. Flávio's dances, performed at pre-campaign events, are part of a strategy to soften the Bolsonaro family's image among voters, set to the song 'Zero Um, Novo Capitão'. Gayer also faces PF indictment over suspected misuse of parliamentary funds.

item.title: Os 60 anos do MDB e os dilemas do centro
item.description: The MDB turns 60 as a party born from the dictatorship that became a pillar of Brazil’s redemocratization, yet the political center now struggles to assert itself in a polarized landscape. Despite spawning parties like the PSDB and influencing the PSD, the center has not elected a president since FHC, and current options such as Caiado, Ratinho Júnior, and Eduardo Leite face perceptions of weakness and the effectiveness of aggressive rhetoric in public discourse.

item.title: Ala do governo avalia Medida Provisória de fim da escala 6x1 se debate da PEC ‘empacar’ na Câmara
item.description: The Lula government is considering issuing a Provisional Measure to end the 6x1 work schedule if the constitutional amendment (PEC) on reducing working hours stalls in the Chamber’s CCJ for two weeks. The move aims to boost electoral prospects ahead of the October elections but risks legal challenges in the Supreme Court over urgency and relevance criteria. Officials prefer an urgency bill that would lock the legislative agenda if not voted within 45 days, preventing opposition from burying the proposal.
